name: Spiced Tomato & Egg Curry
summary: A rich tomato and egg curry made with simple pantry staples and fresh spices.
steps: Heat oil and sauté chopped onions until translucent.
Add garlic, tomatoes, spices and simmer until the sauce thickens.
Crack eggs into the curry and cook until the whites are firm.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with bread or rice.
chefTips: Use ripe tomatoes for a richer curry base.
Simmer slowly so the flavors deepen without burning the spices.
Add a splash of water if the sauce becomes too thick.

name: Paneer Tomato Skillet
summary: A quick skillet recipe with paneer, tomatoes and warm Indian spices.
steps: Cube the paneer and lightly pan-fry until golden.
Cook onions, tomatoes and spices into a thick gravy.
Stir in the paneer cubes and simmer for 5 minutes.
Finish with fresh coriander and serve warm.
chefTips: Avoid overcooking paneer to keep it soft and tender.
Add a pinch of sugar to balance tomato acidity.
Use a nonstick pan for easier cleanup.

name: Vegetable Maggi Stir Fry
summary: A playful stir fry that transforms instant noodles into a satisfying vegetable meal.
steps: Boil the noodles until just tender and drain.
Sauté vegetables with spices until crisp-tender.
Toss the cooked noodles with the vegetables and seasoning.
Serve immediately with a squeeze of lemon.
chefTips: Cook vegetables over high heat to preserve texture.
Keep the noodles slightly undercooked before stir-frying.
Add fresh herbs or chili flakes for extra brightness.

name: Onion Tomato Egg Scramble
summary: A bright, protein-packed scramble that turns pantry ingredients into a flavorful breakfast or dinner.
steps: Sauté onions until soft, then add tomatoes and spices.
Pour beaten eggs into the pan and stir gently.
Cook until the eggs are set but still moist.
Serve with toast or warm flatbread.
chefTips: Use medium heat to avoid drying out the eggs.
Finish with fresh coriander or a dash of lemon juice.
Add a little butter for extra richness.
